planned_launches: Banque Directe launch (April 2026) — new direct banking entity for digital sales
Managed Hosting migration (July 2026) — move from self-hosted to MH Enterprise 2
CLO activation (H2 2026) — activate 500K inactive customers via AI-powered campaigns
Family Banking, Pockets, TVP (2027+) — Ignite workshop roadmap items

scope.ai_analytics_plans: CLO for behavioral segmentation, next-best-offer, campaign orchestration targeting 500K inactive customers
scope.services_scope: Existing managed services contract covers implementation capacity

client_concerns: CIO is price-sensitive — got 65%+ discounts on current contract
Adding €1M+ to existing €1.3M/yr spend feels like doubling the bill
Budget needs steer-co approval — CIO defends but loses interest if numbers too high
Procurement doesn't interfere but CEO asks 'where is my cut' at the end
sensitivities: CIO needs to feel he negotiated well — leave room for a visible win
MH + DA pricing already shown at €751,659/yr — that number is anchored
CLO pricing NOT yet shared — this is the key variable
Current contract has 65%+5% discount — client expects similar treatment
Chinabank Philippines CLO model (fixed $140K + variable take rate) is a viable precedent
constraints.notes: Two genuinely different options: Option 1 = Activation Partnership (low fixed CLO ~€150K + variable take rate on outcomes, total fixed ~€900K) inspired by Chinabank CLO model. Option 2 = Growth Bundle (all-inclusive flat ~€1.15M/yr, no variable). CIO narrative for Option 1: 'Under €1M fixed, we earn when you earn.' MH+DA already at €751K so CLO is the swing factor. Walk-away is €1M ARR. Opening at €1.15-1.4M depending on option. Business case shows €4.8M/yr value at stake — investment pays for itself even at 25% confidence.
